title: Fixing Broken HTML Anchor Tag 'rel' Attributes with Invalid Values

statement.markdown: Write a JavaScript function that validates and normalizes the 'rel' attribute values in HTML anchor tags. The function should accept a string representing the HTML anchor tag as input and return a boolean indicating whether the 'rel' attribute is valid. The function should support the following valid 'rel' values: 'noopener', 'noreferrer', 'noopener noreferrer', and 'noreferrer noopener'. Any other 'rel' value should be considered invalid.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the HTML anchor tag.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the 'rel' attribute is valid.
statement.notes: The function should ignore any other attributes in the anchor tag and only validate the 'rel' attribute.

constraints.description: The number of HTML anchor tags to process.

hints.text: Start by parsing the 'rel' attribute from the anchor tag.

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing Broken HTML Anchor Tag 'rel' Attributes
editorial.intuition: The key to this problem is to correctly parse the 'rel' attribute from the anchor tag and then check if it matches one of the valid values.
editorial.bruteForce: You can use a regular expression to extract the 'rel' attribute from the anchor tag and then check the value manually.
editorial.optimalSolution: You can use a regular expression to extract the 'rel' attribute from the anchor tag and then check the value against a set of valid values.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution has a time complexity of O(n) and a space complexity of O(n), where n is the length of the anchor tag.
editorial.dryRun: Let's run the optimal solution on the first example: Link.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to handle the case where the 'rel' attribute is missing.
Using a regular expression that matches too much of the anchor tag.
